1.0.2: Fixed "Check for Updates" sometimes doing nothing after finding a new version
Replaced Sparkle's update dialog with a native prompt that shows the new version and its changelog before installing

Added default admin credentials in Settings — save a shared local and/or remote admin username to auto-fill deletion, password reset, and Add Remote Host forms (the password is never saved and must always be entered by hand)
Added drag-to-reorder for remote hosts — swipe a host right, right-click it and choose "Reorder Host", or use the new Reorder button above the list to drag any host to a new position
Release notes now animate smoothly when switching between versions in the Version Info window
Fixed remote host swipes sometimes sliding straight through to the wrong side's buttons, and made the swipe commit distance a bit more deliberate
The remote host setup wizard now saves the host to the sidebar the moment setup succeeds, instead of waiting on a separate confirmation step — closing the window afterward can no longer lose it
Added a soft scroll fade to the remote host list, matching release notes
1.0.1: Fixed the auto-update feed — a stray double-hyphen in the appcast made "Check for Updates" fail with a parsing error for every user
Add Remote Host now opens the setup wizard directly instead of an extra manual-entry screen; the wizard also lets you pick the sidebar icon and colour
Fixed several icons that were too dark to read in dark mode
Added a version picker to the Version Info window so you can browse release notes from previous versions
Softened the scroll fade on release notes so it no longer overlaps the first line
Toned down the colour tint on remote host rows in the sidebar for a lighter look
1.0.0: Native macOS rewrite — replaces the cross-platform Avalonia / clearusers.py tool with a purpose-built Swift app
Account categories — sidebar organises accounts into Students, K-3 Shared, Staff, Office, Admin, and System/Protected for targeted bulk actions
Account details — each account shows its username, UID, and home folder size so you can identify accounts at a glance without opening a terminal
Protected accounts — system accounts and the current operator are locked from deletion with a visible reason tooltip
Flexible deletion modes — delete the account record only, the home folder only, or both; set a default in Settings and override per row as needed
Password reset — set a new password and clear lockouts caused by too many failed login attempts, for both local and remote Macs; works alongside deletions in the same batch run
Preview Mode — simulates the full run (deletions and password resets) and reports exactly what would happen without making any changes
No password prompts — operations run through a privileged XPC helper approved once at install; no admin credentials or sudo passwords are ever typed during normal use
Remote Mac management — connect to any macOS host over SSH and manage its accounts the same way as local ones
Logged-in detection — accounts with an active session on a remote Mac are locked and cannot be deleted while in use

PIN security — set a PIN to protect the app on shared student computers; lock the Remote Hosts tab and/or Settings behind it; stored in /Users/Shared/ so the lock applies to all user accounts on the Mac
Logging — every run is written to ~/Library/Logs/AccountManager/ for audit trails; logs are pruned automatically after 100 runs
Automatic updates — built-in updater checks for new releases in the background and prompts when one is available
Legacy deletion tool — the original dscl -delete and rm -rf method is available as an opt-in toggle in Settings for compatibility, but is no longer used by default
